mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in nonfarm payrolls (PAYEMS) reflect a robust labor market that supports consumer spending and corporate earnings, leading to positive S&P 500 forward returns over a 20-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If payrolls decline month-over-month in 2 of the last 6 months, or if the signal's Rank IC over rolling 2-year windows is below 0.03.

mechanism_narrative: When the federal funds rate drops below 0.5%, it indicates an extremely accommodative monetary stance that compresses discount rates and supports equity valuations, producing positive forward returns.
failure_condition: If forward returns are negative in more than 60% of triggered instances over any 3-year rolling window.

mechanism_narrative: Simultaneous acceleration in industrial production (INDPRO) and real GDP growth (GDPC1) signals strong supply-side momentum that boosts corporate profits and leads to positive forward returns over a 60-trading-day horizon.
failure_condition: If the combined signal yields negative forward returns in more than 50% of periods over a 5-year out-of-sample test.

mechanism_narrative: In a low-unemployment, high-retail-sales environment (UNRATE < 4%, RSAFS > 600000), the economy is likely in a late-cycle phase where excess demand leads to inflationary pressures and eventual monetary tightening, causing negative S&P 500 forward returns over 60 trading days.
failure_condition: If forward returns are positive in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 3-year rolling window, the hypothesis is rejected.

mechanism_narrative: A sharp rise in the unemployment rate (increase of 0.3 percentage points or more within three months) signals weakening labor market conditions that reduce consumer confidence and spending, leading to negative equity returns over the next month.
failure_condition: If forward returns are positive in more than 50% of instances when the trigger fires over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: The difference between the federal funds rate and inflation (real interest rate) determines the relative attractiveness of equities. When inflation exceeds the policy rate, negative real rates encourage investors to shift from bonds to equities, boosting stock prices.
failure_condition: If forward returns are negative in more than 50% of periods with negative real rates over a 5-year window.

mechanism_narrative: Consistent month-over-month increases in housing starts (HOUST) reflect improving builder confidence and future economic activity, acting as a leading indicator for positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If HOUST declines for two consecutive months and forward returns are positive, or if the signal's IC over a 1-year rolling window is below 0.05.

mechanism_narrative: When the month-over-month change in industrial production exceeds 0.5%, it signals accelerating manufacturing activity that boosts corporate earnings and investor sentiment, leading to positive short-term equity returns.
failure_condition: If forward returns are negative in more than 50% of triggered instances over a 2-year rolling window.
